provisions.title: I
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 101. (a) None of the funds provided in title I of this Act, or provided by previous appropriations Acts to the agencies or entities funded in titl
notes: Subsection (a)(1)-(5) are general reprogramming restrictions requiring prior Committee approval
Subsection (a)(6): Investigations reprogramming limits—25% of base up to $150,000 for bases over $100,000; $25,000 limit for bases under $100,000

Subsection (a)(7): Construction reprogramming limits—15% of base up to $3,000,000 for bases over $2,000,000; $300,000 limit for bases under $2,000,000; $3,000,000 for settled claims/changed conditions
Subsection (a)(8): O&M—unlimited for emergencies with notification; 15% of base up to $5,000,000 for bases over $1,000,000; $150,000 for bases under $1,000,000
Subsection (a)(9): MR&T follows same guidelines as (6)-(8) for respective portions
Subsection (a)(10): FUSRAP—up to 15% of base of receiving project
Subsection (b): No reprogramming submissions under $50,000
Subsection (c): Continuing authorities program exempt from (a)(1)

provisions.description: Makes appropriations in Title I available for expenditure or transfer (within each bureau or office) for emergency reconstruction, replacement, or repair of aircraft, buildings, utilities, or other facilities damaged by fire, flood, storm, or other unavoidable causes. Requires that Department emergency-specific funds be exhausted first and that all funds used be replenished by supplemental appropriation.

notes: General provision authorizing emergency use of Title I appropriations

provisions.title: I
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 102. The Secretary of the Interior may authorize the expenditure or transfer of any no year appropriation in this title, in addition to the amounts
notes: Heading: EMERGENCY TRANSFER AUTHORITY—DEPARTMENT-WIDE
Proviso 1: Wildland fire operations appropriations available for prior-year obligations and reimbursement to other Federal agencies for destroyed vehicles/aircraft/equipment
Proviso 2: No funds available until Secretary determines wildland fire suppression funds will be exhausted within 30 days
Proviso 3: All funds must be replenished by supplemental appropriation
Proviso 4: Replenishment funds shall reimburse on pro rata basis accounts from which funds were transferred
References 7 U.S.C. 7717(b) for grasshopper/Mormon cricket authority and section 410 of P.L. 95-87 for emergency reclamation

provisions.description: Establishes nonrefundable Outer Continental Shelf inspection fees for FY2026 to be deposited in the Offshore Safety and Environmental Enforcement account. Sets tiered fee schedules for above-waterline facilities ($10,500/$17,000/$31,500 based on well count), drilling rigs ($30,500/$16,700 based on water depth), and non-rig unit well operations ($13,260/$11,530/$4,470 based on water depth). Specifies billing and payment timelines.

SEC. 107. 
(a) In fiscal year 2026, the Secretary of the Interior shall collect a nonrefundable inspection fee, which shall be deposited in the ''Offsho
notes: Heading: OUTER CONTINENTAL SHELF INSPECTION FEES

Subsection (b): Facility fees — $10,500 (no wells w/ equipment), $17,000 (1-10 wells), $31,500 (>10 wells)
Subsection (c): Drilling rig fees — $30,500 (≥500 ft depth), $16,700 (<500 ft depth)
Subsection (e): Billing and payment timelines specified

(a)

notes: Heading: ALLOCATION OF PROJECTS, LAND AND WATER CONSERVATION FUND
Very complex section with subsections (a) through (e)
Subsection (a)(1): Directs allocation per explanatory statement table; excludes BLM/FWS/NPS Land Acquisition Projects (handled in (a)(2))

Subsection (a)(2): Requires project lists for BLM/FWS/NPS land acquisition; supplementary allocations must total at least half of each account's full amount; expenditure is treated as reprogramming subject to SEC. 421
Subsection (a)(3): Requires 30-day notice to Appropriations Committees before expenditure outside specific tracts described in project data sheets
Subsection (b): LIMITATION — Neither the President nor his designee may allocate LWCF amounts other than as specified in this section, except for continuing program administration
Subsection (c): Requires supplementary allocation lists concurrent with FY2027 budget; project data sheets; willing seller and appraisal requirements; quarterly balance reports
Subsection (d): Directs allocation of FY2025 revised LWCF amounts per explanatory statement
Subsection (e): Establishes parallel process for National Parks and Public Land Legacy Restoration Fund for BLM, FWS, NPS, BIE, and Forest Service

Provisions take effect 30 days after OMB determines uniform ethics rules apply.
